name: bitstream-studio
description: Bitstream Studio — Sensor Telemetry and Sensor Studio (BS2)

commands.title: Open TERNION Web App in Browser

ternion.ws.brokerPort.description: TCP port for the Bitstream / serial WebSocket broker (combined bridge serial side).

ternion.ws.modelBrokerPort.description: TCP port for the Model Loader, Free Loader, and catalog WebSocket broker (separate from serial to avoid contention).

ternion.ws.aiBrokerPort.description: TCP port for the AI bridge WebSocket server (LLM + MCP bitstream tools).

ternion.browserApp.port.description: TCP port for the local static server used by "Open Digital Twin in Browser". Use 0 for an ephemeral (OS-assigned) port.

ternion.project4.mockMcuPort.description: TCP port for the bundled HTTP mock MCU (/data, /move, /setSpeed). Match Project 4 connection MCU base URL (e.g. http://127.0.0.1:<port>).

ternion.mqtt.configuration.description: MQTT client configuration including connection profiles, UI state, and publish history

ternion.githubToken.description: GitHub personal access token (read-only repo scope is enough) for Free Loader list/sync. Raises GitHub API rate limits vs anonymous requests. If empty, GITHUB_TOKEN from the environment is used.

ternion.assets.sourceStrategy.description: Asset source policy for model/cubemap URLs in webview/browser mode: local-only, local-first, or online-only.

scripts.start:inner: concurrently --prefix-colors "cyan,blue,magenta,yellow" --prefix "name" --names "bridge,dev,ai,model" --kill-others-on-fail false "npm run start:bridge" "npm run dev:all" "npm run ai:bridge:no-serial" "npm run start:model-downloader-bridge"
scripts.start:inner:bitstream-ai: concurrently --prefix-colors "cyan,blue,magenta,yellow" --prefix "name" --names "bridge,dev,ai,model" --kill-others-on-fail false "npm run start:bridge" "npm run dev:all" "npm run ai:bridge" "npm run start:model-downloader-bridge"
scripts.start:with-bitstream-mcp: npm run prestart && npm run start:inner:bitstream-ai
scripts.start:inner:no-ai: concurrently --prefix-colors "cyan,blue,yellow" --prefix "name" --names "bridge,dev,model" --kill-others-on-fail false "npm run start:bridge" "npm run dev:all" "npm run start:model-downloader-bridge"
scripts.dev:all: concurrently --prefix-colors "blue,green" --prefix "name" --names "watch,webview" --kill-others-on-fail false --group "npm run watch:all" "npm run dev:webview"
scripts.bitstream:mcp:gate: npm run -s bitstream:mcp:validate && npm run -s test:bitstream2 && npm run -s bitstream:mcp:smoke
scripts.dev:model-loader-browser: concurrently --prefix-colors "cyan,blue" --prefix "name" --names "model-bridge,browser" --kill-others-on-fail false "npm run start:model-downloader-bridge" "npm run dev:browser"
scripts.dev:with-model-loader: concurrently --prefix-colors "cyan,blue,green" --prefix "name" --names "model-bridge,dev" --kill-others-on-fail false "npm run start:model-downloader-bridge" "npm run dev"
scripts.dev:with-mock-mcu: concurrently --prefix-colors "yellow,green" --prefix "name" --names "mock-mcu,dev" --kill-others-on-fail false "npm run project4:mock-mcu" "npm run dev"
scripts.dev:with-t3d-watch: concurrently --prefix-colors "magenta,blue,green" --prefix "name" --names "t3d,watch,webview" --kill-others-on-fail false --group "npm --prefix ../T3D run watch:lib" "npm run watch:all" "npm run dev:webview"
scripts.prewatch:all: node scripts/ensure-t3d-linked-build-fresh.js && node scripts/sync-jolt-assets.js && node scripts/sync-t3d-css.js
scripts.prebuild:webview: node scripts/ensure-t3d-linked-build-fresh.js && node scripts/sync-jolt-assets.js && node scripts/sync-t3d-css.js
scripts.predev:webview: node scripts/ensure-t3d-linked-build-fresh.js && node scripts/sync-jolt-assets.js && node scripts/sync-t3d-css.js
scripts.predev:browser: node scripts/ensure-t3d-linked-build-fresh.js && node scripts/sync-jolt-assets.js && node scripts/sync-t3d-css.js
scripts.rebuild:full: cd ../.. && npm run build:lib && cd apps/t3d-extension && npm run compile && npx vsce package --no-dependencies --allow-missing-repository
scripts.package: npm run compile && npx vsce package --no-dependencies --allow-missing-repository
scripts.publish: npm run compile && npx vsce publish --no-dependencies --allow-missing-repository
scripts.release:patch: npm run version:patch && npm run publish
scripts.release:minor: npm run version:minor && npm run publish
scripts.release:major: npm run version:major && npm run publish
scripts.pretest: npm run compile && npm run lint
scripts.bitstream:ci:check: npm run test:bitstream2 && npm run bitstream:sync:check

description:scripts.dev: Extension watch + Vite only — no Model Downloader WebSocket (ws://localhost:9998). Browser Model Loader needs a bridge; use dev:with-model-loader or dev:model-loader-browser.
description:scripts.dev:with-model-loader: Same as dev plus start:model-downloader-bridge (WS + bridge) so browser Model Loader can connect.
description:scripts.dev:with-mock-mcu: Same as npm run dev plus project4:mock-mcu (HTTP mock at 127.0.0.1:8787) for Project 4 without hardware.
description:scripts.dev:with-t3d-watch: Run T3D watch:lib + extension/webview dev together so T3D dist updates automatically during coding.
description:scripts.dev:model-loader-browser: Lightweight browser dev: Vite + model-downloader bridge only (no extension TS watch).
description:scripts.dev:clean: Try to free dev TCP ports (default 9998 broker; set DEV_CLEAN_PORTS=9998,5173). Use --dry-run to only check. Cross-platform.
description:scripts.prestart: Runs automatically before npm start to free default broker port(s) so start is less likely to hit EADDRINUSE.
description:scripts.start:no-clean: Same as npm start but skips prestart/dev:clean (use when broker port is already free or another tool owns it).
description:scripts.start:inner:bitstream-ai: Like start:inner but the AI lane runs **npm run ai:bridge** (Bitstream HostSession attach) instead of ai:bridge:no-serial. Set BITSTREAM_SERIAL_PATH (or rely on auto-detect). See DEVELOPMENT_COMMANDS.md.
description:scripts.start:with-bitstream-mcp: **npm run prestart** then **start:inner:bitstream-ai** — one command for broker + dev + MCP-capable AI bridge (Sensor Studio Bitstream tools). Not the same as **npm start** (supervisor + no-serial AI).
description:scripts.start:bridge: Combined broker + serial bridge (one-shot; restart manually after bridge TS edits)
description:scripts.start:bridge:watch: Same as start:bridge but tsx watch — auto-restart on bridge entry / serialport-bridge edits
description:scripts.start:bridge:attach: Attach only bridge clients to an already-running ws://127.0.0.1:9998 server (avoids EADDRINUSE from start:bridge).
description:scripts.dev:browser: Run webview standalone in browser (fast dev)
description:scripts.dev:bitstream2-loopback: Bridge + Vite for Simulator telemetry; run bitstream-simulator extension alongside. Browser opens /?app=bitstream.
description:scripts.compile: Full build: extension + webview for VS Code
description:scripts.ai:bridge: WebSocket server for Assistant / Ai Dev Trace (default port 9987). Included in npm start as ai:bridge:no-serial to avoid COM contention with start:bridge.
description:scripts.bitstream:assistant: Alias for ai:bridge. For firmware-backed MCP tools without a second serial attach, run this alone or stop start:bridge — see src/ai/README.md.
description:scripts.ai:bridge:no-serial: Same as ai:bridge but skips serial attach (ANTHROPIC-only smoke or UI without COM).
description:scripts.publish:studio-asset-manifest: PUT bundled `studio-asset-manifest.v1.json` to `ternion-3d-assets-free` (`assets/studio-asset-manifest.v1.json`). Requires env `GITHUB_TOKEN` (repo scope). Optional: `GITHUB_MANIFEST_OWNER`, `GITHUB_MANIFEST_REPO`, `GITHUB_MANIFEST_BRANCH`.
description:scripts.sync:studio-cubemap-assets: HTTP GET cubemap JPEGs from `ternion-3d-assets-free` into `src/assets/textures/cubemap/...`. Default: paths from bundled `studio-asset-manifest.v1.json`. `--discover-all`: GitHub Contents API lists all `assets/textures/cubemap/*` (like `node-animator/scripts/download-assets.ts`); optional `GITHUB_TOKEN` for API rate limit. `--dry-run`, `--force`. Env: `STUDIO_ASSETS_RAW_BASE`, `GITHUB_FREE_ASSETS_*`. For private repos use Git Bash + SSH where your key works.
